projectDescription: A Spring Boot + Netty TCP server that receives real-time GPS data from GT06 protocol devices and forwards it to a backend service via HTTP.
keyFeatures: TCP server on port 9000 accepting GT06 protocol GPS device connections
GT06 binary protocol parsing: login, location, and heartbeat packets
GPS coordinate conversion (raw integer to decimal degrees with hemisphere support)
Heartbeat ACK responses to keep devices connected
HTTP forwarding of parsed GPS data to a configurable backend service
Spring Boot Actuator integration for health monitoring
publicApiSurface: POST /data/ubicacion — Receive GPS location data (JSON body: GPSData)
TCP port 9000 — GT06 protocol binary socket for GPS device connections
Protocol 0x13 — Heartbeat packet handler (ACC status, GSM signal)
GET /actuator — Spring Boot Actuator health and metrics endpoints
GPSData model: imei, timestamp, latitud, longitud, velocidad, gpsValido, acc, corteMotor
GT06Utils.decodificarIMEI(byte[]) — Decode IMEI from BCD bytes
GT06Utils.calcularCRC(byte[]) — CRC-16 checksum for packet validation
GT06Utils.ACK(short serial) — Build login acknowledgement packet
GT06Utils.ACKHeartbeat(short serial) — Build heartbeat acknowledgement packet
